canonical_truth.summary: A patch of bare earth near the brook is venting warmth from below — soil microbes have crossed the late-winter threshold and the ground is beginning to ferment ahead of the canopy. The first ramp shoot of the year has broken the surface in the warm patch.
canonical_truth.location: south of the brook crossing, the bare-earth patch where the snow lifted first
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: Underground warmth surfaces as a smell and a single green spear; the patch becomes a foragable point one sense ahead of the rest of the woods.

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: A patch of bare earth near the brook smells different — not cold mud, but something warmer, a faint ferment rising from below, the way stored food smells when it is just crossing into ripe.

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: Scientists develop a fuel cell powered by microbes in ordinary soil that produces electricity to run underground sensors entirely from within — the discovery, reported on 2026-05-03, that living soil generates usable energy without batteries or sun.
authorial_note.translation_logic: Energy generated from within the soil translates to YY noticing the ground itself waking — warmth and ferment-smell rising from below, surfacing as the first green of the year. The discovery's underground-and-self-sustaining quality becomes a sensory turn YY can step into.
authorial_note.restraint_reason: The science arrives as a smell and a shoot, never as a concept. YY does not understand microbes or batteries; he understands warmth-from-below, and the green spear that confirms it.
